mnemonics.title: Cyclic Group Generator
mnemonics.content: C-GET: Cyclic Group = Every element is Generated by Exponent Transforming from one element (the generator)

mnemonics.title: Symmetric vs Alternating
mnemonics.content: S-NAP: Symmetric = All Permutations, Alternating = N-only-A-Principal Even permutations

acronyms.expansion: Symmetric group of degree n - All n! permutations
Alternating group of degree n - All even permutations
acronyms.usage: Even permutations of n objects, order n!/2

acronyms.expansion: Dihedral group of order 2n - Symmetries of n-gon
acronyms.usage: Rotations and reflections of regular polygon

acronyms.expansion: Unit group modulo n - Invertible elements under mod n multiplication
acronyms.usage: Multiplicative group of integers relatively prime to n, order φ(n)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a clock face - starting from 12 (identity), moving by 1 hour repeatedly cycles through all 12 positions. That's a cyclic group Z12.
Picture a square: rotate it (0°, 90°, 180°, 270°) and flip it along 4 axes (vertical, horizontal, two diagonals) - that's D4.
Think of 3 differently colored balls - all 6 ways to arrange them: identity, 3 transpositions, 2 3-cycles.

analogies.analogy: Like days of the week - after Sunday comes Monday, after Saturday comes Sunday again. The pattern repeats endlessly.
Like a rectangle's symmetries: do nothing, flip horizontal, flip vertical, flip both (which equals rotate 180°). All order 2.
Like shuffling a deck of cards - S52 is all possible shuffles, A52 is only the 'even' shuffles (cannot be done by single swap).
